comment: Injected on top of source.jeth (which is TypeScript) so JETH-specific tokens are colored inside every context (class bodies, type annotations, expressions). 'L:' gives these higher priority than the host TypeScript rules; comments and strings are excluded.

jeth-decorators.comment: The KEEP-list decorators - the only ones legal in native-only JETH (@payable is constructor-only; the rest per SUPPORTED.md 'Legacy decorator removal').

jeth-decorators-retired.comment: The 21 REMOVED legacy decorators (JETH481) plus @hidden (JETH440) - colored as deprecated so a retired spelling is visibly wrong in the editor before compiling. Native replacements: bare class / abstract / type P={} / interface / static class / External<T> / Visible<T> / inferred mutability / #private / bare fields / static K / event<{}> / error<{}> / indexed<T> / receive() / fallback().

jeth-decorators-user.comment: A user-named decorator = a modifier APPLICATION (@onlyOwner, @mark(7n)) - legal on methods when a matching @modifier is declared. Matched after the two specific rules above.

jeth-markers.comment: The native value markers that replaced the visibility/mutability decorators: External<T>/Payable<T> on methods, Visible<T> on fields (the public auto-getter), View<T>/Pure<T> in interfaces and on get accessors (declared mutability). These names are RESERVED_MARKERS in the language (JETH038), so coloring every occurrence is faithful.
jeth-markers.name: support.type.marker.jeth storage.modifier.jeth

jeth-type-constructors.comment: The event/error/indexed TYPE CONSTRUCTORS (X: event<{...}>, E: error<{...}>, x: indexed<T>), colored only when followed by '<' so plain identifiers named event/error stay untouched.
jeth-type-constructors.name: support.type.constructor.jeth storage.modifier.jeth

jeth-types.comment: Sized integers u8..u256 / i8..i256 (step 8), bytes1..bytes32, bytes/bool/address/string/void, and the type constructors mapping / Arr / Brand.
jeth-types.name: support.type.jeth

jeth-global-members.comment: Members of the global objects, colored only after their namespace + a dot so user identifiers named e.g. `value` are unaffected.

jeth-builtins.comment: Free builtin functions / operators (not generic member names, to avoid mis-coloring user variables).
jeth-builtins.name: support.function.builtin.jeth
